mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals economic deceleration, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with restrictive monetary policy creates stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing downward over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by 50 bps annually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate expanding consumer demand and construction sector activity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with HOUST levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index rises above 305.0, persistent price pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and depressing forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (adjusted R-squared delta < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of late-cycle GDP deceleration episodes, or the signal predictive power vanishes after adjusting for market sentiment indicators.
